name: TCP Transport Protocol
description: In-depth quiz on the TCP protocol: reliability, flow control, congestion control and handshaking.

questions.question: Unlike UDP, what is the nature of the delivery service offered by a TCP connection?
options: It transfers data without prior setup, dispatching packets to the destination.
It guarantees the delivery of a continuous, ordered and reliable byte stream.
It delegates to the network layer the vital task of correcting corrupted data.
It prioritizes minimal network latency by dropping all old packets.

questions.question: TCP implements rigorous Flow Control using the 'rwnd' (receive window) field. What is its function?
options: It natively prevents core routers from losing crucial packets along the route.
It requires the speed of the physical cable to slow down to match the card's capacity.
It ensures that high-traffic applications do not overload the core CPUs.
It prevents the sender's send rate from overflowing the receiver's buffer.

questions.question: How do Sequence Numbers operate within the TCP segment header?
options: They report the identifier of the logical port allocated to that process.
They represent the numbering of the first data byte contained in the segment.
They sequentially indicate the total number of packets already transmitted.
They define the processing priority of the packet within the IP router.

questions.question: What does the TCP protocol do if it receives 3 duplicate ACKs (Fast Retransmit)?
options: It promptly retransmits the suspect packet even before suffering the timeout.
It clears its congestion window and restarts the communication from scratch.
It immediately triggers slow start, sending only a single MSS.
It sends a stop signal to the router to relieve the bottlenecks along the path.

questions.question: In TCP's Slow Start mechanism, how does the Congestion Window (cwnd) behave initially?
options: It expands its window linearly, adding one segment per RTT cycle.
It restricts the speed to a single byte in order to empty out routers.
It doubles the size of its congestion window every elapsed RTT.
It demands that the client application limit the generation of multimedia flows.

questions.question: Why does TCP's classic congestion control adopt the behavior known as AIMD ('sawtooth')?
options: To encrypt the transferred flow by scrambling the total sizes of the packets.
To be able to reserve in advance all the bandwidth on the nodes along the path.
To probe the network channel, adjusting the rate until it finds its loss limit.
To avoid hardware interrupts caused by the high processing of data.

questions.question: Why does creating a TCP connection require the '3-way handshake' instead of a simple two-message exchange?
options: Because the IP protocol demands this mandatory step in its own architecture.
Because the link layer requires double checks against data collision on the wire.
Because it is necessary to transfer the security keys before the data begins.
Because delayed or reordered messages could create faulty connections on the host.

questions.question: What is the exact semantics of the 'Acknowledgement Number' field sent by the TCP receiver?
options: It carries the sequence number of the next byte the receiver expects to receive.
It contains the total count of all datagrams already dropped during routing.
It signals the total number of segments successfully sent in the whole session.
It records the exact value of the propagation delay measured in the current transmission.

questions.question: Considering the concept of fairness, is TCP considered a 'fair' protocol for dividing bandwidth at a bottleneck?
options: No, because the protocol always favors audio and video traffic over textual traffic.
Yes, as long as the competing connections present the same RTT delay on the network.
Yes, since the edge routers block flows that exceed their quota.
No, because newer connections receive absolute priority in the output buffers.

questions.question: How does TCP handle the network's delay variations when defining its Timeout (EstimatedRTT)?
options: It adopts solely and strictly the exact value of the last RTT just measured.
It delegates this complex calculation to the IP protocol, which sees the global path.
It uses the Exponentially Weighted Moving Average to avoid abrupt fluctuations.
It defines the time limit by mathematically dividing the size of a document.
